relations.note: Charts and tables bypass the asymmetric accent corner rule to apply a uniform 12px radius.

quotes: A rectangle with **selectively enlarged opposite corner radii**

large: 120px & 6px; banner: 48px & 6px; callout: 42px & 6px. Apply the large radius to one diagonal pair (e.g., top-left + bottom-right), the 6px radius to the other pair. Be consistent within an email

rules.intent: Maintain the distinct, optimistic brand container device across digital layouts consistently.
rules.rule_text: Apply the asymmetric accent shape (selectively enlarged opposite corner radii) to callout boxes, banners, and large containers. For email (96dpi), large containers/photos use 120px & 6px diagonal radii; banners use 48px & 6px; and small callouts use 42px & 6px (represented by Accent Box Radius (radius.container.callout_box = 40px 8px 40px 8px)). The diagonal orientation must remain consistent within an email (defaulting to the large radius on top-left and bottom-right corners).

quotes: Exception: charts and tables use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (not the asymmetric accent shape). [GENERAL: 12px uniform radius.]

rules.intent: Ensure data presentations like charts and tables remain clear and legible with balanced, uniform borders.
rules.rule_text: Charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners on all four corners (uniform corner radius: 12px), acting as an exception to the asymmetric accent shape design.
